Application to amend the Official Plan and Zoning By-law to permit a mixed-use development consisting of four residential buildings 44, 39, 27, and 13-storeys, a new park at the northwest corner of the site, a courtyard privately owned publicly-accessible space, and a mid-block connection. This is a substantially amended Zoning Amendment application submitted to address City concerns regardng the previous proposal for a 56-storey residential building.  The application has been appealed to the Ontario Municipal Board (OMB).  The revised proposal is for a 23-storey commerical building with retail and office uses with an overall height (including the mechanical penthouse) of 103.5 metres.  Vehicular parking is proposed to be located below grade, with 31 spaces to be provided in a four level automated parking system.

Zoning By-law and Official Plan Amendment application to permit a 14 storey mixed use building and a 12-storey mixed use building. Vehicle access through the site is proposed via a driveway from Avenue Road and a new public road between Lawrence Avenue West and Douglas Avenue. A public park is proposed at the north end of the site.The proposal would have 665 residential dwelling units and a total of approximately 61,581 square metres of gross floor area including 4,438 square metres of retail space. The proposed Floor Space Index is 4.06. 703 vehicle parking spaces and 521 bicycle parking spaces are proposed.

The application proposes four stacked townhouse blocks, each 3-1/2 storeys in height, comprised of 82 residential units, with a total residential gross floor area (GFA) of 9,023 square metres. The proposed development includes 96 parking spaces within a shared underground garage.

Site Plan Application for the second phase of the Newtonbrook Plaza development.  Block 2 proposes a total gross floor area of 66,838.9 square metres, consisting of 63,152.3 square metres of residential GFA and 3,628.0 square metres of commercial GFA.  The proposed mixed use building would contain two towers on a shared podium. One tower will be 34 storeys (106.3 metres excluding the mechanical penthouse) and the other proposed tower would be 40 storeys (123.5 metres excluding the mechanical penthouse). A total of 856 residential units are proposed with 543 vehicular parking spaces.  A new community centre and child care centre are also proposed as part of Phase 2.

OPA & Rezoning Application proposes the development of a 13-storey seniors residence providing residential suites, assisted living suites and memory care suites. Combined Official Plan & Zoning By-law Amendment for a new 63-storey mixed-use building, containing approximately 357 dwelling units and 702.8 sq. m. of non-residential gross floor area

Site Plan Control Application proposes the development of a 13-storey residential retirement building. The building will provide a total of 208 residential units, being a mix of studio, 1-bedroom, 1 bedroom + den, and 2-bedroom. The retirement residence will offer a range of living options, including memory care, assisted living, independent living, and rental replacement units. A paved access from Alexandra Blvd along the west property line will be provided, leading to 1 level of below-grade parking, providing a total of 24 parking spaces; as well as 35 bicycle parking spaces. The proposed development provides a total GFA of 20,860 square metres and a FSI of 6.93. Additionally, the proposed development provides 3,325.00 square metres of indoor amenity space and 1,023.00 square metres of outdoor amenity space.
This Official Plan Amendment application proposes to re-designate the site from "Core Employment Areas" to "Mixed Use Areas" designation to permit a future mixed-use development.

Phased Site Plan Control application to construct a two-storey warehouse addition to the existing industrial building. Phase 1 will have a GFA of 2,410m² and Phase 2 will have a GFA of 3,294m². The total GFA, including the existing industrial building, will be 10,780m².
